Perfect. **LRECL = 2265** for `DSU0059.CLMS`.

Now the fix is clear. Instead of reading line by line (which splits on `\x0A`), we read **exactly 2265 bytes at a time** in binary mode.

But we don't want to hardcode `2265` — we should get it from config so it works for any combined file.

---

## Step 1: Add `lrecl` to YAML

```yaml name=config-dev.yaml
          combined_file_routing:
            lrecl: 2265             # ← add this - fixed logical record length from mainframe spec
            doc_type:
              position: 17
            routes:
              ...
```

---

## Step 2: Update routing pass in `combined_copybook_ingestion_utils.py`

The **only thing that changes** is how we read the source file during the routing pass — from text-mode line-by-line to binary-mode fixed-length chunks.

Replace the entire routing pass section:

```python name=src/utils/mainframe/combined_copybook_ingestion_utils.py
    # Read lrecl from config - mandatory for combined files with computational fields
    lrecl = routing_cfg.get("lrecl")
    if not lrecl:
        raise ValueError(
            f"{module_name} - combined_file_routing requires 'lrecl' (logical record length). "
            f"Check mainframe spec or measure a known good record in the file."
        )
    lrecl = int(lrecl)
```

Then replace the chunk reading loop:

```python name=src/utils/mainframe/combined_copybook_ingestion_utils.py
    # We do NOT use split_file_by_lines here because that uses text mode
    # which would split on \x0A bytes inside computational fields.
    # Instead we read the original file in binary mode using fixed lrecl.
    # Chunk files from pff_file_splitter are also read in binary mode below
    # so they are safe too.
    chunk_files = split_file_by_lines(source_file, chunk_size_bytes=chunk_size_bytes)

    get_logger().info(
        f"{module_name} - Starting routing pass. "
        f"source_file={source_file}, chunks={len(chunk_files)}, lrecl={lrecl}"
    )

    for cf in chunk_files:
        with open(cf, "rb") as f:                          # ← binary mode
            while True:
                raw_record = f.read(lrecl)                 # ← read exactly lrecl bytes
                if not raw_record:
                    break                                  # ← end of file

                # Handle last record which may have a trailing \r\n or \n
                # Strip only trailing CR/LF that are outside the lrecl boundary
                # (mainframe files sometimes have \r\n after each record)
                if len(raw_record) < lrecl:
                    # partial read at end of file - skip incomplete record
                    get_logger().warning(
                        f"{module_name} - Partial record at end of {cf} "
                        f"(got {len(raw_record)} bytes, expected {lrecl}). Skipping."
                    )
                    break

                # Decode the fixed-width binary record to string for routing
                # Use cp1252 with replace so any undecodable bytes become ?
                # This matches how _migrate_data_bcp_style_local_csv reads later
                try:
                    line = raw_record.decode("cp1252", errors="replace")
                except Exception as decode_err:
                    get_logger().warning(
                        f"{module_name} - Failed to decode record: {decode_err}. Skipping."
                    )
                    continue

                # Strip only trailing newline characters that mainframe may append
                # after each lrecl block (\r\n or \n) - these are record separators
                # not part of the data
                line = line.rstrip("\r\n")

                if not line:
                    continue

                total_records += 1

                doc_type_val = _char_at_pos_1_based(line, doc_type_pos)
                tail_val = _char_at_pos_1_based(line, tail_pos)

                matched = False

                for route_key, obj in routed_outputs.items():
                    if default_key and route_key == default_key:
                        continue
                    if _matches_route(doc_type_val, tail_val, obj["cfg"], tail_true_values):
                        with open(obj["path"], "a", encoding="cp1252", errors="replace") as wf:
                            wf.write(line + "\n")
                        matched = True
                        break

                if not matched:
                    if default_key:
                        with open(
                            routed_outputs[default_key]["path"], "a",
                            encoding="cp1252", errors="replace"
                        ) as wf:
                            wf.write(line + "\n")
                    else:
                        unmatched_count += 1
                        unmatched_meta.append({
                            "line_number": total_records,
                            "reason": f"No route matched. doc_type='{doc_type_val}' tail_ind='{tail_val}'",
                            "preview": line[:120],
                        })
                        with open(unmatched_path, "a", encoding="cp1252", errors="replace") as uf:
                            uf.write(line + "\n")
```

---

## What this fixes

| Before | After |
|---|---|
| `open(cf, "r")` → splits on `\x0A` inside COMP fields | `open(cf, "rb")` → reads exactly `lrecl` bytes |
| 1 record split into 5-6 lines | 1 record = exactly 2265 bytes always |
| 300k records → 300k+ lines | 300k records → exactly 300k reads |
| Computational field `\x0A` breaks routing | `\x0A` inside record bytes ignored |

---

## One more thing — `pff_file_splitter` also uses binary mode already

Looking at your existing `pff_file_splitter.py`:

```python name=src/utils/mainframe/pff_file_splitter.py
with open(source_file, 'rb') as infile:   # ← already binary
    for line in infile:                    # ← but still splits on \x0A
```

This means `split_file_by_lines` also has the same problem for computational fields — it will split chunk boundaries mid-record. 

For combined files with `lrecl` we should **skip chunking entirely** during routing and just read the original file directly in fixed-length binary mode. The chunking is only useful if the file is > 1GB.

So add this check before the `split_file_by_lines` call:

```python name=src/utils/mainframe/combined_copybook_ingestion_utils.py
    # For combined files with lrecl, skip pff_file_splitter entirely.
    # pff_file_splitter reads line-by-line in binary which still splits on \x0A
    # inside computational fields. We read the original file directly
    # in fixed-length binary mode using lrecl.
    chunk_files = [source_file]   # ← always just the original file
```

This is safe because we are now reading in fixed binary chunks of `lrecl` bytes — not line by line — so the file size does not matter.

references.name: combined_copybook_ingestion_utils.py
references.text:
import json
import os
import time
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple

from src.config.settings import get_settings
from src.copybook.copybook_parser import parse_cobol_xml
from src.utils.config_utils import ConfigUtils
from src.utils.logging_utils import setup_logger, get_logger
from src.utils.mainframe.cobol_to_bcp_convertion import (
    _migrate_data_bcp_style_local_csv,
    _insert_local_csv_to_sql_using_bcp_command,
)
from src.utils.mainframe.pff_file_splitter import split_file_by_lines

logger = setup_logger("pipeline")
config = ConfigUtils()


def _char_at_pos_1_based(line: str, pos: Optional[int]) -> str:
    """
    Return a single character at 1-based position from a fixed-width mainframe line.
    Position 1 = first character of the line (mainframe convention).
    Returns empty string if pos is None or out of range.
    """
    if not pos:
        return ""
    idx = int(pos) - 1
    if idx < 0 or idx >= len(line):
        return ""
    return line[idx]


def _matches_route(
    doc_type_val: str,
    tail_val: str,
    route_cfg: dict,
    tail_true_values: List[str],
) -> bool:
    """
    Check if a line's doc_type and tail_ind values match a given route config.

    Route config supports:
      - doc_type: "U"   → single char match
      - doc_type: "YZ"  → multi-char string meaning "any of Y or Z"
      - tail_ind: "1"   → matches if tail_val is in tail_true_values

    Matching rules:
      - If route has doc_type condition → doc_type_val must be in that string
      - If route has tail_ind condition → tail_val must be in tail_true_values
      - Both conditions must pass if both are present (AND logic)
      - First match wins in the calling loop (most specific routes first in config)
    """
    expected_doc = (route_cfg.get("doc_type") or "").strip()
    expected_tail = (route_cfg.get("tail_ind") or "").strip()

    if expected_doc:
        # "YZ" means doc_type in {'Y', 'Z'}
        if doc_type_val not in list(expected_doc):
            return False

    if expected_tail:
        # tail_ind condition present → check if tail_val is truthy
        if expected_tail in tail_true_values:
            if tail_val not in tail_true_values:
                return False
        else:
            # literal match
            if tail_val != expected_tail:
                return False

    return True


def _resolve_xml_abs_path(xml_rel: str) -> str:
    """
    Resolve absolute path for a copybook XML file.
    Follows the same convention used in sftp_utils:
      os.path.join(get_settings().BASE_DIR, "sql_ingest_copybooks", cobol_copybook_config)
    """
    if os.path.isabs(xml_rel):
        return xml_rel
    # Normalize forward slashes to os.sep to handle mixed separators on Windows
    xml_rel_normalized = xml_rel.replace("/", os.sep)
    return os.path.join(get_settings().BASE_DIR, "sql_ingest_copybooks", xml_rel_normalized)


def ingest_combined_mainframe_file_using_copybooks(
    module_name: str,
    source_file: str,
    source_file_config: dict,
) -> Tuple[int, int, int, int, int, Dict[str, Dict[str, int]]]:
    """
    Ingest ONE downloaded mainframe flat file into MULTIPLE SQL Server tables
    based on routing rules (doc_type and/or tail_ind positions).

    Flow:
      1. Read combined file line by line
      2. For each line extract doc_type and/or tail_ind character values
      3. Match against routes in config order (first match wins - most specific first)
      4. Write matched lines into per-route temporary flat files
      5. Write unmatched lines into unmatched + unmatched.meta.json (continue, do not fail)
      6. For each routed flat file:
         a. parse_cobol_xml  → schema
         b. _migrate_data_bcp_style_local_csv  → per-route CSV (handles parse errors, missed, missed.meta.json)
         c. _insert_local_csv_to_sql_using_bcp_command  → BCP load into target table (handles bcp.err)
      7. Return totals + per-table stats for email message building

    Args:
        module_name:          Job name from config (e.g. INGEST_ALL_CLAIMS)
        source_file:          Absolute path to downloaded mainframe file
        source_file_config:   The sftp_config dict for this file from config YAML

    Returns:
        total_records               - total non-empty lines read from source file
        affected_count_total        - sum of BCP inserted rows across all routes
        non_empty_error_records_total - sum of parse errors + unmatched records
        merge_inserted_total        - same as affected_count_total (reserved for upsert later)
        merge_updated_total         - always 0 (reserved for upsert later)
        per_table_stats             - dict keyed by route key with parsed/affected/error counts
    """
    routing_cfg = source_file_config.get("combined_file_routing") or {}

    # Extract routing positions from config
    doc_type_pos = (routing_cfg.get("doc_type") or {}).get("position")
    tail_cfg = routing_cfg.get("tail_ind") or {}
    tail_pos = tail_cfg.get("position")
    tail_true_values: List[str] = tail_cfg.get("true_values") or ["1"]

    routes = routing_cfg.get("routes") or []
    default_cfg = routing_cfg.get("default")

    # BCP options (same keys as existing sftp_utils convention)
    is_bcp_load_needed = bool(source_file_config.get("is_bcp_load_needed", False))
    alternative_delimiter = bool(source_file_config.get("alternative_delimiter", False))
    quote_char = source_file_config.get("quote_char", "§")
    delimiter_value = source_file_config.get("delimiter_value", "\x1F")
    chunk_size_bytes = int(source_file_config.get("chunk_size_bytes", 1024 * 1024 * 1024))

    if not is_bcp_load_needed:
        raise ValueError(
            f"{module_name} - combined_file_routing requires is_bcp_load_needed=true. "
            f"Combined file ingestion only supports the BCP path."
        )

    if not routes and not default_cfg:
        raise ValueError(
            f"{module_name} - combined_file_routing has no routes and no default defined."
        )

    # Temp folder for routed files and unmatched files
    tmp_folder_prefix = config.get(None, "etl_temp_share.folder_location", "./")
    routed_dir = os.path.join(
        tmp_folder_prefix,
        "combined_mainframe_routed",
        datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d"),
    )
    os.makedirs(routed_dir, exist_ok=True)

    base_name = os.path.basename(source_file)

    # Build route output map: route_key -> {cfg, path}
    # Order is preserved (Python 3.7+ dicts maintain insertion order)
    # Most specific routes must be listed first in config
    routed_outputs: Dict[str, dict] = {}

    for idx, r in enumerate(routes):
        tbl = r.get("target_table_name")
        if not tbl:
            raise ValueError(
                f"{module_name} - combined route at index {idx} is missing target_table_name."
            )
        route_key = f"route_{idx + 1}__{tbl.replace('.', '_').replace('[', '').replace(']', '').replace(' ', '')}"
        out_path = os.path.join(routed_dir, f"{base_name}.{route_key}")
        routed_outputs[route_key] = {"cfg": r, "path": out_path}
        # Truncate / create file fresh
        open(out_path, "w", encoding="cp1252").close()

    # Default route (for VOIDS-style files)
    default_key = None
    if default_cfg:
        tbl = default_cfg.get("target_table_name")
        if not tbl:
            raise ValueError(
                f"{module_name} - combined_file_routing default is missing target_table_name."
            )
        default_key = f"default__{tbl.replace('.', '_').replace('[', '').replace(']', '').replace(' ', '')}"
        out_path = os.path.join(routed_dir, f"{base_name}.{default_key}")
        routed_outputs[default_key] = {"cfg": default_cfg, "path": out_path}
        open(out_path, "w", encoding="cp1252").close()

    # Unmatched records output (routing-level failures only)
    unmatched_path = os.path.join(routed_dir, f"{base_name}.unmatched")
    unmatched_meta_path = os.path.join(routed_dir, f"{base_name}.unmatched.meta.json")
    open(unmatched_path, "w", encoding="cp1252").close()

    total_records = 0
    unmatched_count = 0
    unmatched_meta = []

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Step 1: Route the source file into per-table flat files
    # Chunk the file by byte size to handle very large files (reuses existing splitter)
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    chunk_files = split_file_by_lines(source_file, chunk_size_bytes=chunk_size_bytes)

    get_logger().info(
        f"{module_name} - Starting routing pass. source_file={source_file}, chunks={len(chunk_files)}"
    )

    for cf in chunk_files:
        with open(cf, "r", encoding="cp1252", errors="replace") as f:
            for line_num, line in enumerate(f, start=1):
                line = line.rstrip("\n")
                if not line:
                    continue

                total_records += 1

                doc_type_val = _char_at_pos_1_based(line, doc_type_pos)
                tail_val = _char_at_pos_1_based(line, tail_pos)

                matched = False

                # First match wins - routes evaluated in config order
                # Most specific routes (doc_type + tail_ind) must come first in config
                for route_key, obj in routed_outputs.items():
                    if default_key and route_key == default_key:
                        # Default is only used when nothing else matches - skip in main loop
                        continue
                    if _matches_route(doc_type_val, tail_val, obj["cfg"], tail_true_values):
                        with open(obj["path"], "a", encoding="cp1252", errors="replace") as wf:
                            wf.write(line + "\n")
                        matched = True
                        break

                if not matched:
                    if default_key:
                        # Write to default route (e.g. VOIDS file base table)
                        with open(routed_outputs[default_key]["path"], "a", encoding="cp1252", errors="replace") as wf:
                            wf.write(line + "\n")
                    else:
                        # No route and no default - write to unmatched file and continue
                        unmatched_count += 1
                        unmatched_meta.append({
                            "line_number": line_num,
                            "reason": f"No route matched. doc_type='{doc_type_val}' tail_ind='{tail_val}'",
                            "preview": line[:120],
                        })
                        with open(unmatched_path, "a", encoding="cp1252", errors="replace") as uf:
                            uf.write(line + "\n")

    # Write unmatched metadata JSON
    try:
        with open(unmatched_meta_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as jf:
            json.dump(
                {
                    "source_file": source_file,
                    "unmatched_count": unmatched_count,
                    "entries": unmatched_meta,
                },
                jf,
                ensure_ascii=False,
                indent=2,
            )
    except Exception as meta_err:
        get_logger().warning(
            f"{module_name} - Failed to write unmatched meta file {unmatched_meta_path}: {meta_err}"
        )

    get_logger().info(
        f"{module_name} - Routing pass complete. "
        f"total_records={total_records}, unmatched={unmatched_count}"
    )

    if unmatched_count > 0:
        get_logger().warning(
            f"{module_name} - {unmatched_count} unmatched records written to {unmatched_path}. "
            f"Check {unmatched_meta_path} for details."
        )

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Step 2: For each routed flat file, apply copybook + BCP load
    # Reuses _migrate_data_bcp_style_local_csv and _insert_local_csv_to_sql_using_bcp_command
    # exactly as copybook_parser.upload_pff_with_copybook_to_mssql does in BCP path
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    affected_count_total = 0
    non_empty_error_records_total = unmatched_count  # unmatched are also error records
    merge_inserted_total = 0
    merge_updated_total = 0  # reserved for future upsert support per route

    per_table_stats: Dict[str, Dict[str, int]] = {}

    failed_routes = []  # tracks which routes had BCP or CSV errors
    for route_key, obj in routed_outputs.items():
        cfg = obj["cfg"]
        routed_path = obj["path"]
        target_table = cfg.get("target_table_name")
        xml_rel = cfg.get("cobol_copybook")

        if not xml_rel or not target_table:
            raise ValueError(
                f"{module_name} - Route {route_key} is missing cobol_copybook or target_table_name."
            )

        routed_file_size = os.path.getsize(routed_path)

        if routed_file_size == 0:
            # No records routed to this table - skip BCP entirely
            get_logger().info(
                f"{module_name} - {route_key} has 0 records routed. Skipping BCP load for {target_table}."
            )
            per_table_stats[route_key] = {
                "table": target_table,
                "parsed": 0,
                "affected": 0,
                "non_empty_error_records": 0,
            }
            continue

        xml_abs = _resolve_xml_abs_path(xml_rel)

        if not os.path.exists(xml_abs):
            raise FileNotFoundError(
                f"{module_name} - Copybook XML not found: {xml_abs} for route {route_key}."
            )

        get_logger().info(
            f"{module_name} - Processing route {route_key}. "
            f"table={target_table}, copybook={xml_abs}, routed_file={routed_path}"
        )

        # Parse copybook XML schema (same as existing BCP path in copybook_parser.py)
        schema, expected_length = parse_cobol_xml(xml_abs)

        route_failed = False
        route_error_message = None
        record_count = 0
        inserted_count = 0
        non_empty_error_records = 0

        try:
            csv_filename, record_count, non_empty_error_records = _migrate_data_bcp_style_local_csv(
                routed_path,
                schema,
                module_name,
                target_table,
                alternative_delimiter=alternative_delimiter,
                encoding="cp1252",
                expected_length=expected_length,
                quote_char=quote_char,
                delimiter_value=delimiter_value,
            )
        except Exception as csv_err:
            route_failed = True
            route_error_message = f"CSV generation failed: {csv_err}"
            get_logger().error(
                f"{module_name} - {route_key} CSV generation failed. "
                f"table={target_table}, error={csv_err}. "
                f"Skipping BCP load for this route and continuing with remaining routes."
            )

        if not route_failed:
            try:
                inserted_count = _insert_local_csv_to_sql_using_bcp_command(
                    csv_filename,
                    module_name,
                    target_table,
                    alternative_delimiter=alternative_delimiter,
                    delimiter_value=delimiter_value,
                )
            except Exception as bcp_err:
                route_failed = True
                route_error_message = f"BCP load failed: {bcp_err}"
                get_logger().error(
                    f"{module_name} - {route_key} BCP load failed. "
                    f"table={target_table}, error={bcp_err}. "
                    f"Check bcp.err file for rejected rows. "
                    f"Continuing with remaining routes."
                )

        affected_count_total += inserted_count
        merge_inserted_total += inserted_count
        non_empty_error_records_total += non_empty_error_records

        per_table_stats[route_key] = {
            "table": target_table,
            "parsed": record_count,
            "affected": inserted_count,
            "non_empty_error_records": non_empty_error_records,
            "failed": route_failed,
            "error": route_error_message,
        }

        if route_failed:
            failed_routes.append(route_key)
            get_logger().error(
                f"{module_name} - {route_key} FAILED. "
                f"table={target_table}, reason={route_error_message}"
            )
        else:
            get_logger().info(
                f"{module_name} - {route_key} done. "
                f"table={target_table}, parsed={record_count}, "
                f"affected={inserted_count}, non_empty_errors={non_empty_error_records}"
            )

    # Cleanup chunk files (never delete the original source file)
    for cf in chunk_files:
        if cf != source_file and os.path.exists(cf):
            try:
                os.remove(cf)
            except Exception as e:
                get_logger().warning(f"{module_name} - Failed to delete chunk file {cf}: {e}")

    # Raise at the end ONLY after all routes have been attempted
    if failed_routes:
        raise RuntimeError(
            f"{module_name} - Combined ingestion completed with failures. "
            f"Failed routes: {failed_routes}. "
            f"Check bcp.err and missed files for details."
        )

    get_logger().info(
        f"{module_name} - Combined ingestion complete. "
        f"total_records={total_records}, "
        f"affected_total={affected_count_total}, "
        f"non_empty_error_records_total={non_empty_error_records_total}, "
        f"unmatched={unmatched_count}"
    )

    return (
        total_records,
        affected_count_total,
        non_empty_error_records_total,
        merge_inserted_total,
        merge_updated_total,
        per_table_stats,
    )

```python name=src/utils/mainframe/combined_copybook_ingestion_utils.py
import json
import os

from datetime import datetime
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple

from src.config.settings import get_settings
from src.copybook.copybook_parser import parse_cobol_xml
from src.utils.config_utils import ConfigUtils
from src.utils.logging_utils import setup_logger, get_logger
from src.utils.mainframe.cobol_to_bcp_convertion import (

logger = setup_logger("pipeline")
config = ConfigUtils()


def _char_at_pos_1_based(line: str, pos: Optional[int]) -> str:
    """
    Return a single character at 1-based position from a fixed-width mainframe line.
    Position 1 = first character of the line (mainframe convention).
    Returns empty string if pos is None or out of range.
    """
    if not pos:
        return ""
    idx = int(pos) - 1
    if idx < 0 or idx >= len(line):
        return ""
    return line[idx]


def _matches_route(
    doc_type_val: str,
    tail_val: str,
    route_cfg: dict,
    tail_true_values: List[str],
) -> bool:
    """
    Check if a line's doc_type and tail_ind values match a given route config.

    Route config supports:
      - doc_type: "U"   -> single char match
      - doc_type: "YZ"  -> multi-char string meaning "any of Y or Z"
      - tail_ind: "1"   -> matches if tail_val is in tail_true_values

    Matching rules:
      - If route has doc_type condition -> doc_type_val must be in that string
      - If route has tail_ind condition -> tail_val must be in tail_true_values
      - Both conditions must pass if both are present (AND logic)
      - First match wins in the calling loop (most specific routes first in config)
    """
    expected_doc = (route_cfg.get("doc_type") or "").strip()
    expected_tail = (route_cfg.get("tail_ind") or "").strip()

    if expected_doc:
        # "YZ" means doc_type in {'Y', 'Z'}
        if doc_type_val not in list(expected_doc):
            return False

    if expected_tail:
        # tail_ind condition present -> check if tail_val is in true_values
        if expected_tail in tail_true_values:
            if tail_val not in tail_true_values:
                return False
        else:
            # literal match
            if tail_val != expected_tail:
                return False

    return True


def _resolve_xml_abs_path(xml_rel: str) -> str:
    """
    Resolve absolute path for a copybook XML file.
    Follows the same convention used in sftp_utils:
      os.path.join(get_settings().BASE_DIR, "sql_ingest_copybooks", cobol_copybook_config)
    Normalizes forward slashes to os.sep to handle mixed separators on Windows.
    """
    if os.path.isabs(xml_rel):
        return xml_rel
    xml_rel_normalized = xml_rel.replace("/", os.sep)
    return os.path.join(get_settings().BASE_DIR, "sql_ingest_copybooks", xml_rel_normalized)


def ingest_combined_mainframe_file_using_copybooks(
    module_name: str,
    source_file: str,
    source_file_config: dict,
) -> Tuple[int, int, int, int, int, Dict[str, Dict[str, int]]]:
    """
    Ingest ONE downloaded mainframe flat file into MULTIPLE SQL Server tables
    based on routing rules (doc_type and/or tail_ind positions).

    Flow:
      1. Read source file in BINARY mode using fixed lrecl (logical record length)
         - Binary mode prevents splitting on 0x0A bytes inside COMP fields
         - lrecl must be specified in combined_file_routing config
      2. For each fixed-length record extract doc_type and/or tail_ind character values
      3. Match against routes in config order (first match wins - most specific first)
      4. Write matched records into per-route temporary flat files (no extension, same as pff_file_splitter)
      5. Write unmatched records into unmatched + unmatched.meta.json (continue, do not fail)
      6. For each routed flat file:
         a. parse_cobol_xml  -> schema + expected_length
         b. _migrate_data_bcp_style_local_csv  -> per-route CSV
            (handles parse errors -> missed + missed.meta.json)
         c. _insert_local_csv_to_sql_using_bcp_command  -> BCP load into target table
            (handles BCP rejected rows -> bcp.err)
      7. Per-route failures are caught and logged - remaining routes continue
      8. After all routes attempted - raise RuntimeError if any routes failed
      9. Return totals + per-table stats for email message building

    Args:
        module_name:          Job name from config (e.g. INGEST_CLAIMS)
        source_file:          Absolute path to downloaded mainframe file
        source_file_config:   The sftp_config dict for this file from config YAML

    Returns:
        total_records               - total non-empty records read from source file
        affected_count_total        - sum of BCP inserted rows across all routes
        non_empty_error_records_total - sum of parse errors + unmatched records
        merge_inserted_total        - same as affected_count_total (reserved for upsert later)
        merge_updated_total         - always 0 (reserved for upsert later)
        per_table_stats             - dict keyed by route key with parsed/affected/error counts
    """
    routing_cfg = source_file_config.get("combined_file_routing") or {}

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Validate and read lrecl - mandatory for binary fixed-length reading
    # Prevents splitting on 0x0A bytes inside COMP/binary computational fields
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    lrecl = routing_cfg.get("lrecl")
    if not lrecl:
        raise ValueError(
            f"{module_name} - combined_file_routing requires 'lrecl' (logical record length). "
            f"Check mainframe spec or measure a known good record in the file using Notepad++."
        )
    lrecl = int(lrecl)

    # Extract routing positions from config
    doc_type_pos = (routing_cfg.get("doc_type") or {}).get("position")
    tail_cfg = routing_cfg.get("tail_ind") or {}
    tail_pos = tail_cfg.get("position")
    tail_true_values: List[str] = tail_cfg.get("true_values") or ["1"]

    routes = routing_cfg.get("routes") or []
    default_cfg = routing_cfg.get("default")

    # BCP options (same keys as existing sftp_utils convention)
    is_bcp_load_needed = bool(source_file_config.get("is_bcp_load_needed", False))
    alternative_delimiter = bool(source_file_config.get("alternative_delimiter", False))
    quote_char = source_file_config.get("quote_char", "§")
    delimiter_value = source_file_config.get("delimiter_value", "\x1F")

    if not is_bcp_load_needed:
        raise ValueError(
            f"{module_name} - combined_file_routing requires is_bcp_load_needed=true. "
            f"Combined file ingestion only supports the BCP path."
        )

    if not routes and not default_cfg:
        raise ValueError(
            f"{module_name} - combined_file_routing has no routes and no default defined."
        )

    # Temp folder for routed files and unmatched files
    tmp_folder_prefix = config.get(None, "etl_temp_share.folder_location", "./")
    routed_dir = os.path.join(
        tmp_folder_prefix,
        "combined_mainframe_routed",
        datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d"),
    )
    os.makedirs(routed_dir, exist_ok=True)

    base_name = os.path.basename(source_file)

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Build route output map: route_key -> {cfg, path}
    # Order is preserved (Python 3.7+ dicts maintain insertion order)
    # Most specific routes (doc_type + tail_ind both) must be listed first in config
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    routed_outputs: Dict[str, dict] = {}

    for idx, r in enumerate(routes):
        tbl = r.get("target_table_name")
        if not tbl:
            raise ValueError(
                f"{module_name} - combined route at index {idx} is missing target_table_name."
            )
        route_key = (
            f"route_{idx + 1}__{tbl.replace('.', '_').replace('[', '').replace(']', '').replace(' ', '')}"
        )
        # No file extension - same convention as pff_file_splitter chunk files
        out_path = os.path.join(routed_dir, f"{base_name}.{route_key}")
        routed_outputs[route_key] = {"cfg": r, "path": out_path}
        # Truncate / create file fresh for this run
        open(out_path, "w", encoding="cp1252").close()

    # Default route (for VOIDS-style files where most records go to one table
    # unless a specific condition like tail_ind=1 routes them elsewhere)
    default_key = None
    if default_cfg:
        tbl = default_cfg.get("target_table_name")
        if not tbl:
            raise ValueError(
                f"{module_name} - combined_file_routing default is missing target_table_name."
            )
        default_key = (
            f"default__{tbl.replace('.', '_').replace('[', '').replace(']', '').replace(' ', '')}"
        )
        out_path = os.path.join(routed_dir, f"{base_name}.{default_key}")
        routed_outputs[default_key] = {"cfg": default_cfg, "path": out_path}
        open(out_path, "w", encoding="cp1252").close()

    # Unmatched records output (routing-level failures only)
    # No extension on data file - .meta.json only on metadata file
    unmatched_path = os.path.join(routed_dir, f"{base_name}.unmatched")
    unmatched_meta_path = os.path.join(routed_dir, f"{base_name}.unmatched.meta.json")
    open(unmatched_path, "w", encoding="cp1252").close()

    total_records = 0
    unmatched_count = 0
    unmatched_meta = []

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Step 1: Route the source file into per-table flat files
    #
    # IMPORTANT: Read in BINARY mode with fixed lrecl bytes per record.
    # DO NOT use text mode or pff_file_splitter here.
    # Text mode splits on 0x0A which is a valid byte inside COMP/binary fields.
    # pff_file_splitter also iterates line-by-line in binary which has same problem.
    # Reading exactly lrecl bytes at a time gives us one clean logical record always.
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    get_logger().info(
        f"{module_name} - Starting routing pass. "
        f"source_file={source_file}, lrecl={lrecl}"
    )

    with open(source_file, "rb") as f:                      # binary mode - no line splitting
        record_num = 0
        while True:
            raw_record = f.read(lrecl)                      # read exactly lrecl bytes

            if not raw_record:
                break                                       # end of file

            # Handle record separator bytes that mainframe may append after each record
            # Some mainframe files have \r\n or \n after each lrecl block as record separator
            # These are not part of the data - peek and skip them
            if len(raw_record) == lrecl:
                # Peek at next byte to check for record separator
                next_byte = f.read(1)
                if next_byte == b"\r":
                    # CR found - check for LF after it
                    after_cr = f.read(1)
                    if after_cr != b"\n":
                        # Not CRLF - put it back by seeking back 1
                        f.seek(-1, 1)
                elif next_byte == b"\n":
                    # LF only separator - consumed and discarded
                    pass
                elif next_byte:
                    # Not a separator - seek back so next read starts correctly
                    f.seek(-1, 1)
                # if next_byte is empty - end of file, nothing to do

            if len(raw_record) < lrecl:
                # Partial record at end of file - not a valid complete record
                get_logger().warning(
                    f"{module_name} - Partial record at end of file "
                    f"(got {len(raw_record)} bytes, expected {lrecl}). Skipping."
                )
                break

            record_num += 1

            # Decode binary record to string using cp1252
            # errors="replace" converts undecodable bytes to ? - same as existing BCP path
            try:
                line = raw_record.decode("cp1252", errors="replace")
            except Exception as decode_err:
                get_logger().warning(
                    f"{module_name} - Failed to decode record {record_num}: {decode_err}. Skipping."
                )
                continue

            # Strip only trailing \r\n that are part of the record itself
            # (not the separator we already consumed above - just defensive strip)
            line = line.rstrip("\r\n")

            if not line.strip():
                continue

            total_records += 1

            # Extract routing key values at configured 1-based positions
            doc_type_val = _char_at_pos_1_based(line, doc_type_pos)
            tail_val = _char_at_pos_1_based(line, tail_pos)

            matched = False

            # First match wins - routes evaluated in config order
            # Most specific routes (doc_type + tail_ind both present) must come first in config
            for route_key, obj in routed_outputs.items():
                if default_key and route_key == default_key:
                    # Default route is fallback only - skip in main matching loop
                    continue
                if _matches_route(doc_type_val, tail_val, obj["cfg"], tail_true_values):
                    with open(obj["path"], "a", encoding="cp1252", errors="replace") as wf:
                        wf.write(line + "\n")
                    matched = True
                    break

            if not matched:
                if default_key:
                    # Write to default route (e.g. VOIDS base table)
                    with open(
                        routed_outputs[default_key]["path"], "a",
                        encoding="cp1252", errors="replace"
                    ) as wf:
                        wf.write(line + "\n")
                else:
                    # No route and no default - write to unmatched and continue
                    unmatched_count += 1
                    unmatched_meta.append({
                        "line_number": total_records,
                        "reason": (
                            f"No route matched. "
                            f"doc_type='{doc_type_val}' tail_ind='{tail_val}'"
                        ),
                        "preview": line[:120],
                    })
                    with open(unmatched_path, "a", encoding="cp1252", errors="replace") as uf:
                        uf.write(line + "\n")

    # Write unmatched metadata JSON
    try:
        with open(unmatched_meta_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as jf:
            json.dump(
                {
                    "source_file": source_file,
                    "unmatched_count": unmatched_count,
                    "entries": unmatched_meta,
                },
                jf,
                ensure_ascii=False,
                indent=2,
            )
    except Exception as meta_err:
        get_logger().warning(
            f"{module_name} - Failed to write unmatched meta file {unmatched_meta_path}: {meta_err}"
        )

    get_logger().info(
        f"{module_name} - Routing pass complete. "
        f"total_records={total_records}, unmatched={unmatched_count}"
    )

    if unmatched_count > 0:
        get_logger().warning(
            f"{module_name} - {unmatched_count} unmatched records written to {unmatched_path}. "
            f"Check {unmatched_meta_path} for details."
        )

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Step 2: For each routed flat file, apply copybook schema + BCP load
    # Reuses _migrate_data_bcp_style_local_csv and _insert_local_csv_to_sql_using_bcp_command
    # exactly as copybook_parser.upload_pff_with_copybook_to_mssql does in BCP path
    # Per-route failures are caught - all routes are attempted before raising
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    affected_count_total = 0
    non_empty_error_records_total = unmatched_count  # unmatched are also counted as errors
    merge_inserted_total = 0
    merge_updated_total = 0                          # reserved for future upsert support per route

    per_table_stats: Dict[str, Dict[str, int]] = {}
    failed_routes = []                               # tracks routes that had CSV or BCP errors

    for route_key, obj in routed_outputs.items():
        cfg = obj["cfg"]
        routed_path = obj["path"]
        target_table = cfg.get("target_table_name")
        xml_rel = cfg.get("cobol_copybook")

        if not xml_rel or not target_table:
            raise ValueError(
                f"{module_name} - Route {route_key} is missing cobol_copybook or target_table_name."
            )

        routed_file_size = os.path.getsize(routed_path)

        if routed_file_size == 0:
            # No records routed to this table - skip BCP entirely
            get_logger().info(
                f"{module_name} - {route_key} has 0 records routed. "
                f"Skipping BCP load for {target_table}."
            )
            per_table_stats[route_key] = {
                "table": target_table,
                "parsed": 0,
                "affected": 0,
                "non_empty_error_records": 0,
                "failed": False,
                "error": None,
            }
            continue

        xml_abs = _resolve_xml_abs_path(xml_rel)

        if not os.path.exists(xml_abs):
            raise FileNotFoundError(
                f"{module_name} - Copybook XML not found: {xml_abs} for route {route_key}."
            )

        get_logger().info(
            f"{module_name} - Processing route {route_key}. "
            f"table={target_table}, copybook={xml_abs}, routed_file={routed_path}"
        )

        # Parse copybook XML schema - expected_length comes from XML storage-length attribute
        schema, expected_length = parse_cobol_xml(xml_abs)

        route_failed = False
        route_error_message = None
        record_count = 0
        inserted_count = 0
        non_empty_error_records = 0

        # Generate CSV from routed flat file
        # Handles parse errors -> writes *.missed + *.missed.meta.json automatically
        try:
            csv_filename, record_count, non_empty_error_records = _migrate_data_bcp_style_local_csv(
                routed_path,
                schema,
                module_name,
                target_table,
                alternative_delimiter=alternative_delimiter,
                encoding="cp1252",
                expected_length=expected_length,
                quote_char=quote_char,
                delimiter_value=delimiter_value,
            )
        except Exception as csv_err:
            route_failed = True
            route_error_message = f"CSV generation failed: {csv_err}"
            get_logger().error(
                f"{module_name} - {route_key} CSV generation failed. "
                f"table={target_table}, error={csv_err}. "
                f"Skipping BCP load for this route and continuing with remaining routes."
            )

        # BCP load CSV into target SQL Server table
        # BCP rejected rows are written to *.bcp.err automatically
        if not route_failed:
            try:
                inserted_count = _insert_local_csv_to_sql_using_bcp_command(
                    csv_filename,
                    module_name,
                    target_table,
                    alternative_delimiter=alternative_delimiter,
                    delimiter_value=delimiter_value,
                )
            except Exception as bcp_err:
                route_failed = True
                route_error_message = f"BCP load failed: {bcp_err}"
                get_logger().error(
                    f"{module_name} - {route_key} BCP load failed. "
                    f"table={target_table}, error={bcp_err}. "
                    f"Check bcp.err file for rejected rows. "
                    f"Continuing with remaining routes."
                )

        affected_count_total += inserted_count
        merge_inserted_total += inserted_count
        non_empty_error_records_total += non_empty_error_records

        per_table_stats[route_key] = {
            "table": target_table,
            "parsed": record_count,
            "affected": inserted_count,
            "non_empty_error_records": non_empty_error_records,
            "failed": route_failed,
            "error": route_error_message,
        }

        if route_failed:
            failed_routes.append(route_key)
            get_logger().error(
                f"{module_name} - {route_key} FAILED. "
                f"table={target_table}, reason={route_error_message}"
            )
        else:
            get_logger().info(
                f"{module_name} - {route_key} done. "
                f"table={target_table}, parsed={record_count}, "
                f"affected={inserted_count}, non_empty_errors={non_empty_error_records}"
            )

    get_logger().info(
        f"{module_name} - Combined ingestion complete. "
        f"total_records={total_records}, "
        f"affected_total={affected_count_total}, "
        f"non_empty_error_records_total={non_empty_error_records_total}, "
        f"unmatched={unmatched_count}"
    )

    # Raise at the end ONLY after all routes have been attempted
    # This ensures routes 5 and 6 still run even if route 4 fails
    if failed_routes:
        raise RuntimeError(
            f"{module_name} - Combined ingestion completed with failures. "
            f"Failed routes: {failed_routes}. "
            f"Check bcp.err and missed files for details."
        )

    return (
        total_records,
        affected_count_total,
        non_empty_error_records_total,
        merge_inserted_total,
        merge_updated_total,
        per_table_stats,
    )
```

You are right to question that.

I removed it because I removed `split_file_by_lines` — so no chunk files are ever created during the routing pass. The only files created now are the **per-route routed files** in `combined_mainframe_routed` folder.

The per-route routed files (`DSU0059.CLMS.route_1__dbo_ADO_ADJUSTMENT_CLAIM` etc.) sitting in `combined_mainframe_routed` folder are **temporary** — they should be cleaned up after BCP loading is done, just like chunk files were.

```python name=src/utils/mainframe/combined_copybook_ingestion_utils.py
import json
import os
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple

from src.config.settings import get_settings
from src.copybook.copybook_parser import parse_cobol_xml
from src.utils.config_utils import ConfigUtils
from src.utils.logging_utils import setup_logger, get_logger
from src.utils.mainframe.cobol_to_bcp_convertion import (
    _migrate_data_bcp_style_local_csv,
    _insert_local_csv_to_sql_using_bcp_command,
)

logger = setup_logger("pipeline")
config = ConfigUtils()


def _char_at_pos_1_based(line: str, pos: Optional[int]) -> str:
    """
    Return a single character at 1-based position from a fixed-width mainframe line.
    Position 1 = first character of the line (mainframe convention).
    Returns empty string if pos is None or out of range.
    """
    if not pos:
        return ""
    idx = int(pos) - 1
    if idx < 0 or idx >= len(line):
        return ""
    return line[idx]


def _matches_route(
    doc_type_val: str,
    tail_val: str,
    route_cfg: dict,
    tail_true_values: List[str],
) -> bool:
    """
    Check if a line's doc_type and tail_ind values match a given route config.

    Route config supports:
      - doc_type: "U"   -> single char match
      - doc_type: "YZ"  -> multi-char string meaning "any of Y or Z"
      - tail_ind: "1"   -> matches if tail_val is in tail_true_values

    Matching rules:
      - If route has doc_type condition -> doc_type_val must be in that string
      - If route has tail_ind condition -> tail_val must be in tail_true_values
      - Both conditions must pass if both are present (AND logic)
      - First match wins in the calling loop (most specific routes first in config)
    """
    expected_doc = (route_cfg.get("doc_type") or "").strip()
    expected_tail = (route_cfg.get("tail_ind") or "").strip()

    if expected_doc:
        # "YZ" means doc_type in {'Y', 'Z'}
        if doc_type_val not in list(expected_doc):
            return False

    if expected_tail:
        # tail_ind condition present -> check if tail_val is in true_values
        if expected_tail in tail_true_values:
            if tail_val not in tail_true_values:
                return False
        else:
            # literal match
            if tail_val != expected_tail:
                return False

    return True


def _resolve_xml_abs_path(xml_rel: str) -> str:
    """
    Resolve absolute path for a copybook XML file.
    Follows the same convention used in sftp_utils:
      os.path.join(get_settings().BASE_DIR, "sql_ingest_copybooks", cobol_copybook_config)
    Normalizes forward slashes to os.sep to handle mixed separators on Windows.
    """
    if os.path.isabs(xml_rel):
        return xml_rel
    xml_rel_normalized = xml_rel.replace("/", os.sep)
    return os.path.join(get_settings().BASE_DIR, "sql_ingest_copybooks", xml_rel_normalized)


def ingest_combined_mainframe_file_using_copybooks(
    module_name: str,
    source_file: str,
    source_file_config: dict,
) -> Tuple[int, int, int, int, int, Dict[str, Dict[str, int]]]:
    """
    Ingest ONE downloaded mainframe flat file into MULTIPLE SQL Server tables
    based on routing rules (doc_type and/or tail_ind positions).

    Flow:
      1. Read source file in BINARY mode using fixed lrecl (logical record length)
         - Binary mode prevents splitting on 0x0A bytes inside COMP fields
         - lrecl must be specified in combined_file_routing config
      2. For each fixed-length record extract doc_type and/or tail_ind character values
      3. Match against routes in config order (first match wins - most specific first)
      4. Write matched records into per-route temporary flat files
         (no extension - same convention as pff_file_splitter chunk files)
      5. Write unmatched records into unmatched + unmatched.meta.json (continue, do not fail)
      6. For each routed flat file:
         a. parse_cobol_xml  -> schema + expected_length
         b. _migrate_data_bcp_style_local_csv  -> per-route CSV
            (handles parse errors -> missed + missed.meta.json)
         c. _insert_local_csv_to_sql_using_bcp_command  -> BCP load into target table
            (handles BCP rejected rows -> bcp.err)
      7. Per-route failures are caught and logged - remaining routes continue
      8. Cleanup per-route routed files after all BCP loads are done
         (unmatched and unmatched.meta.json are kept for investigation)
      9. After all routes attempted - raise RuntimeError if any routes failed
      10. Return totals + per-table stats for email message building

    Args:
        module_name:          Job name from config (e.g. INGEST_CLAIMS)
        source_file:          Absolute path to downloaded mainframe file
        source_file_config:   The sftp_config dict for this file from config YAML

    Returns:
        total_records               - total non-empty records read from source file
        affected_count_total        - sum of BCP inserted rows across all routes
        non_empty_error_records_total - sum of parse errors + unmatched records
        merge_inserted_total        - same as affected_count_total (reserved for upsert later)
        merge_updated_total         - always 0 (reserved for upsert later)
        per_table_stats             - dict keyed by route key with parsed/affected/error counts
    """
    routing_cfg = source_file_config.get("combined_file_routing") or {}

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Validate and read lrecl - mandatory for binary fixed-length reading
    # Prevents splitting on 0x0A bytes inside COMP/binary computational fields
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    lrecl = routing_cfg.get("lrecl")
    if not lrecl:
        raise ValueError(
            f"{module_name} - combined_file_routing requires 'lrecl' (logical record length). "
            f"Check mainframe spec or measure a known good record in the file using Notepad++."
        )
    lrecl = int(lrecl)

    # Extract routing positions from config
    doc_type_pos = (routing_cfg.get("doc_type") or {}).get("position")
    tail_cfg = routing_cfg.get("tail_ind") or {}
    tail_pos = tail_cfg.get("position")
    tail_true_values: List[str] = tail_cfg.get("true_values") or ["1"]

    routes = routing_cfg.get("routes") or []
    default_cfg = routing_cfg.get("default")

    # BCP options (same keys as existing sftp_utils convention)
    is_bcp_load_needed = bool(source_file_config.get("is_bcp_load_needed", False))
    alternative_delimiter = bool(source_file_config.get("alternative_delimiter", False))
    quote_char = source_file_config.get("quote_char", "§")
    delimiter_value = source_file_config.get("delimiter_value", "\x1F")

    if not is_bcp_load_needed:
        raise ValueError(
            f"{module_name} - combined_file_routing requires is_bcp_load_needed=true. "
            f"Combined file ingestion only supports the BCP path."
        )

    if not routes and not default_cfg:
        raise ValueError(
            f"{module_name} - combined_file_routing has no routes and no default defined."
        )

    # Temp folder for routed files and unmatched files
    tmp_folder_prefix = config.get(None, "etl_temp_share.folder_location", "./")
    routed_dir = os.path.join(
        tmp_folder_prefix,
        "combined_mainframe_routed",
        datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d"),
    )
    os.makedirs(routed_dir, exist_ok=True)

    base_name = os.path.basename(source_file)

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Build route output map: route_key -> {cfg, path}
    # Order is preserved (Python 3.7+ dicts maintain insertion order)
    # Most specific routes (doc_type + tail_ind both) must be listed first in config
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    routed_outputs: Dict[str, dict] = {}

    for idx, r in enumerate(routes):
        tbl = r.get("target_table_name")
        if not tbl:
            raise ValueError(
                f"{module_name} - combined route at index {idx} is missing target_table_name."
            )
        route_key = (
            f"route_{idx + 1}__{tbl.replace('.', '_').replace('[', '').replace(']', '').replace(' ', '')}"
        )
        # No file extension - same convention as pff_file_splitter chunk files
        out_path = os.path.join(routed_dir, f"{base_name}.{route_key}")
        routed_outputs[route_key] = {"cfg": r, "path": out_path}
        # Truncate / create file fresh for this run
        open(out_path, "w", encoding="cp1252").close()

    # Default route (for VOIDS-style files where most records go to one table
    # unless a specific condition like tail_ind=1 routes them elsewhere)
    default_key = None
    if default_cfg:
        tbl = default_cfg.get("target_table_name")
        if not tbl:
            raise ValueError(
                f"{module_name} - combined_file_routing default is missing target_table_name."
            )
        default_key = (
            f"default__{tbl.replace('.', '_').replace('[', '').replace(']', '').replace(' ', '')}"
        )
        out_path = os.path.join(routed_dir, f"{base_name}.{default_key}")
        routed_outputs[default_key] = {"cfg": default_cfg, "path": out_path}
        open(out_path, "w", encoding="cp1252").close()

    # Unmatched records output (routing-level failures only)
    # No extension on data file - .meta.json only on metadata file
    unmatched_path = os.path.join(routed_dir, f"{base_name}.unmatched")
    unmatched_meta_path = os.path.join(routed_dir, f"{base_name}.unmatched.meta.json")
    open(unmatched_path, "w", encoding="cp1252").close()

    total_records = 0
    unmatched_count = 0
    unmatched_meta = []

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Step 1: Route the source file into per-table flat files
    #
    # IMPORTANT: Read in BINARY mode with fixed lrecl bytes per record.
    # DO NOT use text mode or pff_file_splitter here.
    # Text mode splits on 0x0A which is a valid byte inside COMP/binary fields.
    # pff_file_splitter also iterates line-by-line in binary which has same problem.
    # Reading exactly lrecl bytes at a time gives us one clean logical record always.
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    get_logger().info(
        f"{module_name} - Starting routing pass. "
        f"source_file={source_file}, lrecl={lrecl}"
    )

    with open(source_file, "rb") as f:                      # binary mode - no line splitting
        record_num = 0
        while True:
            raw_record = f.read(lrecl)                      # read exactly lrecl bytes

            if not raw_record:
                break                                       # end of file

            if len(raw_record) < lrecl:
                # Partial record at end of file - not a valid complete record
                get_logger().warning(
                    f"{module_name} - Partial record at end of file "
                    f"(got {len(raw_record)} bytes, expected {lrecl}). Skipping."
                )
                break

            record_num += 1

            # Handle record separator bytes that mainframe may append after each lrecl block
            # Some mainframe files have \r\n or \n after each record as separator
            # These are not part of the data - peek and skip them
            next_byte = f.read(1)
            if next_byte == b"\r":
                # CR found - check for LF after it (CRLF separator)
                after_cr = f.read(1)
                if after_cr != b"\n":
                    # Not CRLF - seek back 1 so next read starts correctly
                    f.seek(-1, 1)
            elif next_byte == b"\n":
                # LF only separator - consumed and discarded
                pass
            elif next_byte:
                # Not a separator - seek back so next read starts correctly
                f.seek(-1, 1)
            # if next_byte is empty - end of file, nothing to do

            # Decode binary record to string using cp1252
            # errors="replace" converts undecodable bytes to ? - same as existing BCP path
            try:
                line = raw_record.decode("cp1252", errors="replace")
            except Exception as decode_err:
                get_logger().warning(
                    f"{module_name} - Failed to decode record {record_num}: {decode_err}. Skipping."
                )
                continue

            # Strip only trailing \r\n that are part of the record itself
            # (not the separator we already consumed above - just defensive strip)
            line = line.rstrip("\r\n")

            if not line.strip():
                continue

            total_records += 1

            # Extract routing key values at configured 1-based positions
            doc_type_val = _char_at_pos_1_based(line, doc_type_pos)
            tail_val = _char_at_pos_1_based(line, tail_pos)

            matched = False

            # First match wins - routes evaluated in config order
            # Most specific routes (doc_type + tail_ind both present) must come first in config
            for route_key, obj in routed_outputs.items():
                if default_key and route_key == default_key:
                    # Default route is fallback only - skip in main matching loop
                    continue
                if _matches_route(doc_type_val, tail_val, obj["cfg"], tail_true_values):
                    with open(obj["path"], "a", encoding="cp1252", errors="replace") as wf:
                        wf.write(line + "\n")
                    matched = True
                    break

            if not matched:
                if default_key:
                    # Write to default route (e.g. VOIDS base table)
                    with open(
                        routed_outputs[default_key]["path"], "a",
                        encoding="cp1252", errors="replace",
                    ) as wf:
                        wf.write(line + "\n")
                else:
                    # No route and no default - write to unmatched and continue
                    unmatched_count += 1
                    unmatched_meta.append({
                        "line_number": total_records,
                        "reason": (
                            f"No route matched. "
                            f"doc_type='{doc_type_val}' tail_ind='{tail_val}'"
                        ),
                        "preview": line[:120],
                    })
                    with open(unmatched_path, "a", encoding="cp1252", errors="replace") as uf:
                        uf.write(line + "\n")

    # Write unmatched metadata JSON
    try:
        with open(unmatched_meta_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as jf:
            json.dump(
                {
                    "source_file": source_file,
                    "unmatched_count": unmatched_count,
                    "entries": unmatched_meta,
                },
                jf,
                ensure_ascii=False,
                indent=2,
            )
    except Exception as meta_err:
        get_logger().warning(
            f"{module_name} - Failed to write unmatched meta file {unmatched_meta_path}: {meta_err}"
        )

    get_logger().info(
        f"{module_name} - Routing pass complete. "
        f"total_records={total_records}, unmatched={unmatched_count}"
    )

    if unmatched_count > 0:
        get_logger().warning(
            f"{module_name} - {unmatched_count} unmatched records written to {unmatched_path}. "
            f"Check {unmatched_meta_path} for details."
        )

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Step 2: For each routed flat file, apply copybook schema + BCP load
    # Reuses _migrate_data_bcp_style_local_csv and _insert_local_csv_to_sql_using_bcp_command
    # exactly as copybook_parser.upload_pff_with_copybook_to_mssql does in BCP path
    # Per-route failures are caught - all routes are attempted before raising
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    affected_count_total = 0
    non_empty_error_records_total = unmatched_count  # unmatched are also counted as errors
    merge_inserted_total = 0
    merge_updated_total = 0                          # reserved for future upsert support per route

    per_table_stats: Dict[str, Dict[str, int]] = {}
    failed_routes = []                               # tracks routes that had CSV or BCP errors

    for route_key, obj in routed_outputs.items():
        cfg = obj["cfg"]
        routed_path = obj["path"]
        target_table = cfg.get("target_table_name")
        xml_rel = cfg.get("cobol_copybook")

        if not xml_rel or not target_table:
            raise ValueError(
                f"{module_name} - Route {route_key} is missing cobol_copybook or target_table_name."
            )

        routed_file_size = os.path.getsize(routed_path)

        if routed_file_size == 0:
            # No records routed to this table - skip BCP entirely
            get_logger().info(
                f"{module_name} - {route_key} has 0 records routed. "
                f"Skipping BCP load for {target_table}."
            )
            per_table_stats[route_key] = {
                "table": target_table,
                "parsed": 0,
                "affected": 0,
                "non_empty_error_records": 0,
                "failed": False,
                "error": None,
            }
            continue

        xml_abs = _resolve_xml_abs_path(xml_rel)

        if not os.path.exists(xml_abs):
            raise FileNotFoundError(
                f"{module_name} - Copybook XML not found: {xml_abs} for route {route_key}."
            )

        get_logger().info(
            f"{module_name} - Processing route {route_key}. "
            f"table={target_table}, copybook={xml_abs}, routed_file={routed_path}"
        )

        # Parse copybook XML schema - expected_length comes from XML storage-length attribute
        schema, expected_length = parse_cobol_xml(xml_abs)

        route_failed = False
        route_error_message = None
        record_count = 0
        inserted_count = 0
        non_empty_error_records = 0

        # Generate CSV from routed flat file
        # Handles parse errors -> writes *.missed + *.missed.meta.json automatically
        try:
            csv_filename, record_count, non_empty_error_records = _migrate_data_bcp_style_local_csv(
                routed_path,
                schema,
                module_name,
                target_table,
                alternative_delimiter=alternative_delimiter,
                encoding="cp1252",
                expected_length=expected_length,
                quote_char=quote_char,
                delimiter_value=delimiter_value,
            )
        except Exception as csv_err:
            route_failed = True
            route_error_message = f"CSV generation failed: {csv_err}"
            get_logger().error(
                f"{module_name} - {route_key} CSV generation failed. "
                f"table={target_table}, error={csv_err}. "
                f"Skipping BCP load for this route and continuing with remaining routes."
            )

        # BCP load CSV into target SQL Server table
        # BCP rejected rows are written to *.bcp.err automatically
        if not route_failed:
            try:
                inserted_count = _insert_local_csv_to_sql_using_bcp_command(
                    csv_filename,
                    module_name,
                    target_table,
                    alternative_delimiter=alternative_delimiter,
                    delimiter_value=delimiter_value,
                )
            except Exception as bcp_err:
                route_failed = True
                route_error_message = f"BCP load failed: {bcp_err}"
                get_logger().error(
                    f"{module_name} - {route_key} BCP load failed. "
                    f"table={target_table}, error={bcp_err}. "
                    f"Check bcp.err file for rejected rows. "
                    f"Continuing with remaining routes."
                )

        affected_count_total += inserted_count
        merge_inserted_total += inserted_count
        non_empty_error_records_total += non_empty_error_records

        per_table_stats[route_key] = {
            "table": target_table,
            "parsed": record_count,
            "affected": inserted_count,
            "non_empty_error_records": non_empty_error_records,
            "failed": route_failed,
            "error": route_error_message,
        }

        if route_failed:
            failed_routes.append(route_key)
            get_logger().error(
                f"{module_name} - {route_key} FAILED. "
                f"table={target_table}, reason={route_error_message}"
            )
        else:
            get_logger().info(
                f"{module_name} - {route_key} done. "
                f"table={target_table}, parsed={record_count}, "
                f"affected={inserted_count}, non_empty_errors={non_empty_error_records}"
            )

    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Cleanup per-route routed files after all BCP loads are done
    # Never delete the original source file
    # Keep unmatched and unmatched.meta.json for investigation
    # Keep *.missed, *.missed.meta.json, *.bcp.err - managed by BCP utilities
    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    for route_key, obj in routed_outputs.items():
        routed_path = obj["path"]
        if routed_path != source_file and os.path.exists(routed_path):
            try:
                os.remove(routed_path)
            except Exception as e:
                get_logger().warning(
                    f"{module_name} - Failed to delete routed file {routed_path}: {e}"
                )

    get_logger().info(
        f"{module_name} - Combined ingestion complete. "
        f"total_records={total_records}, "
        f"affected_total={affected_count_total}, "
        f"non_empty_error_records_total={non_empty_error_records_total}, "
        f"unmatched={unmatched_count}"
    )

    # Raise at the end ONLY after all routes have been attempted
    # This ensures all routes run even if one fails
    if failed_routes:
        raise RuntimeError(
            f"{module_name} - Combined ingestion completed with failures. "
            f"Failed routes: {failed_routes}. "
            f"Check bcp.err and missed files for details."
        )

    return (
        total_records,
        affected_count_total,
        non_empty_error_records_total,
        merge_inserted_total,
        merge_updated_total,
        per_table_stats,
    )
```
